title: Arthur the King Review - IndieWire C+ Grade
text: IndieWire's Ryan Lattanzio, Executive Editor, grades Arthur the King with a C+ rating. The film stars Mark Wahlberg as adventure racer Michael Light (based on real-life Swedish racer Mikael Lindnord) who bonds with a stray dog named Arthur during a 435-mile race across the Dominican Republic. Lattanzio characterizes the movie as blandly comforting and notes that it aims straight down the middle of the audience for a mildly reassuring experience mostly made with families in mind. The reviewer observes that Wahlberg has shifted toward making inspirational rather than challenging films in recent years. The critic acknowledges the film's emotional appeal, particularly regarding the bond between man and dog, but finds the human characters lack depth. He writes that the characters are less human beings than people pushing themselves to the brink on a global racetrack. While the dog's performance and the heartwarming finale provide sentimental value, the film lacks psychological insight into the adventure racing experience. Lattanzio suggests watching it on a flight, implying it's suitable lightweight entertainment rather than substantive cinema.
